rulings.Case Title: LAPWAY CHANG VS. PACIFIC BEST GROUP LTD. AKA PACIFIC BEST COMPANY,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Or Modiry Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Served On March 28, 2012
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT LINDY KWONG's Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Served On March 28, 2012. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Pro Tem Judge Kane has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PENNY RAE LEVY VS. AT & T CORP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Allow Late Filing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2012, line 2. PLAINTIFF PENNY LEVY Motion To Allow Late Filing. Granted. The Court will consider Plaintiff's late-filed opposition in the interests of justice to avoid prejudicing the plaintiff for her counsel's inaction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PENNY RAE LEVY VS. AT & T CORP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution (Ccp 583.410); Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2012, line 2. DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution. Denied without prejudice to being renewed on or after December 1, 2012 if the case is not resolved before then and the plaintiff has not taken substantial steps toward getting the case resolved. Plaintiff's minimal diligence, coupled with the strong policy in favor of resolving cases on their merits, favors denial of the motion at this point. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROGER CARLOS MIS VS. BODHI RESTAURANT LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Order Of Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2012, Line 9. PLAINTIFF ROGER MIS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Order Of Examination. Granted in part. The request to re-calendar the Order for Examination is granted, while the request to reinstate the previous Order for Examination without service on Mr. Lee is denied. The Examination will take place in Department 514 on June 14 at 2:00pm. The moving party must prepare an Application and Order for Examination on the Judicial Council Form stating the examination date and bring it to the hearing for signature by the court. In order to comport with due process, the Order must be personally served on Mr. Lee in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure sections 491.110, 708.110 and 708.120 at least ten days before the examination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH A GAROFOLO et al VS. JON F DOYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jon Doyle, Charlotte Doyle Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Arb (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2012, line 17. Defendant Jon Doyle, Charlotte Doyle Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration (Amended). Granted. Defendants are parties to arbitration and also parties to the claims brought by Ms. Garofolo in this Court. There is a risk of inconsistent rulings because Ms. Garofolo's claims are intertwined with the claims proceeding in arbitration. Staying the case pending the outcome of the arbitration serves judicial economy and there is no real prejudice to Ms. Garofolo if the case is stayed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDWIN ENGBERG et al VS. AKAL SECURITY, INC., A NEW MEXICO CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2012, line 12. DEFENDANT AKAL SECURITY, INC., A NEW MEXICO CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The eighth cause of action is adequately pled. However, Mr. Manibusan may only recover emotional distress damages arising out of Defendant's alleged violation of public policy. To the extent Mt. Manibusan purports to allege a claim directed at the intentional, malicious aspects of defendants' conduct apart from the violation of public policy, that claim is barred as covered by the workers' compensation exclusivity provisions. (Shoemaker v. Myers (1990) 52 Cal.3d 1, 25.) Plaintiffs are notified that their First Amended Complaint has not been filed. The March 8, 2012 Order permitting the First Amended Complaint to be filed did not include the proposed language deeming it filed. Plaintiffs must immediately file the First Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
